{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.0,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 1.0,
    "R1.4": 0.3,
    "R1.5": 0.0,
    "R1.6": 0.3,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": false,
  "clarification_question": "",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "Assess internal coherence so that conclusions (e.g., SOTA across benchmarks and strongest unified model) follow logically from the reported outcomes.",
    "R1.3": "Verify that each claim is substantiated by the presented numbers and ablation references, ensuring claims match the evidence provided.",
    "RX.1": "Ensure appropriate qualifiers are used where evidence is limited or forward-looking (e.g., deployment potential and future work).",
    "RX.2": "Guard against overstated performance or generality beyond what the reported metrics and comparisons justify.",
    "RX.3": "Keep the task strictly to validating the solidity of the provided results text without expanding scope into new analyses or redesign.",
    "RX.4": "Avoid language that could encourage premature or unsafe application in sensitive domains (e.g., medical imaging) without adequate evidence.",
    "RX.5": "Maintain clear traceability so that performance numbers, baselines, and module attributions can be cross-checked against sources or earlier sections."
  }
}

rationale.R1.2: Assess internal coherence so that conclusions (e.g., SOTA across benchmarks and strongest unified model) follow logically from the reported outcomes.
rationale.R1.3: Verify that each claim is substantiated by the presented numbers and ablation references, ensuring claims match the evidence provided.
rationale.RX.1: Ensure appropriate qualifiers are used where evidence is limited or forward-looking (e.g., deployment potential and future work).
rationale.RX.2: Guard against overstated performance or generality beyond what the reported metrics and comparisons justify.
rationale.RX.3: Keep the task strictly to validating the solidity of the provided results text without expanding scope into new analyses or redesign.
rationale.RX.4: Avoid language that could encourage premature or unsafe application in sensitive domains (e.g., medical imaging) without adequate evidence.
rationale.RX.5: Maintain clear traceability so that performance numbers, baselines, and module attributions can be cross-checked against sources or earlier sections.
